seoTitle: Enter Form 1095-A in TurboTax Without Errors
metaDescription: Learn how to enter Form 1095-A into TurboTax step by step, including how to gather the right details and avoid common data-entry mistakes.
intro: Getting the numbers on Form 1095-A right in TurboTax matters because they directly feed into your Premium Tax Credit calculation. This guide walks through gathering the information you need and entering it into TurboTax, with a focus on avoiding the data-entry mistakes that trip up self-employed filers and families receiving subsidies. It's useful whether you're filing your own return or assisting a client.

steps.heading: Gather key information from Form 1095-A
steps.body: Locate the monthly premium, second-lowest cost silver plan, and advance payment figures on your form before entering anything.

steps.heading: Open the corresponding section in TurboTax
steps.body: Navigate to the health coverage entry area within TurboTax where 1095-A data is reported.

steps.heading: Enter data month by month
steps.body: Input the figures exactly as listed for each month on your form, since totals can vary if your coverage changed during the year.

steps.heading: Avoid common errors
steps.body: Watch for transposed numbers or skipped months, which are among the most frequent mistakes when entering this form.

steps.heading: Confirm your results
steps.body: Review the calculated credit or repayment amount TurboTax generates to make sure it reflects your actual coverage situation.

faq.question: What details from Form 1095-A does TurboTax need?
faq.answer: It needs the monthly premium amounts, the second-lowest cost silver plan premium, and any advance payments of the premium tax credit.

faq.question: What's a common mistake when entering Form 1095-A?
faq.answer: Entering totals instead of monthly figures, or skipping a month where coverage changed, is a frequent source of errors.

faq.question: Does self-employment income affect how Form 1095-A is used?
faq.answer: Yes, self-employed filers may need to reconcile the Premium Tax Credit differently, so extra care with entry is worthwhile.

faq.question: What if my entered data doesn't match my expected credit?
faq.answer: Double-check each monthly entry against your form first, since even a small mismatch can change your calculated credit.
